currency.name: Angolan kwanza (AOA)
currency.note: Currency has been volatile historically. USD widely used for major transactions alongside kwanza.

crypto.note: No specific regulations but growing interest. Banking infrastructure makes crypto adoption challenging for most users.

article:
🧭 **Overview**
Angola is a vast Southern African nation emerging from decades of civil war (1975-2002) with massive oil wealth but stark inequality. The country spans from Atlantic coastline through savanna to dense forests in the north. Luanda, the capital, is notoriously expensive due to oil industry presence. Portuguese colonial legacy is strong in language and architecture. Economic diversification beyond oil remains a challenge, but infrastructure is improving and younger generations are driving change.

👥 **People & vibe**
With ~35 million people across multiple ethnic groups (Ovimbundu, Kimbundu, Bakongo and others), Angola is culturally diverse. Portuguese is the official language and lingua franca. The population is young (median age ~17) and urbanizing rapidly. Luanda concentrates much of the country's economic activity and expat population. Angolans are generally welcoming but economic disparities create visible tensions. Music (particularly Kizomba and Semba) is a major cultural force. Family and community ties are strong.

[[MAP]]

🌦️ **Climate & landscape**
The climate varies by region: coastal areas are hot and arid moderated by the Benguela Current; the north is tropical and humid; the central highlands (including Huambo) are cooler and temperate. Rainy season runs roughly November to April. The landscape is diverse — Atlantic beaches, expansive savannas, lush forests in Cabinda, and dramatic highlands. Infrastructure varies wildly — Luanda is modern (if chaotic), but rural areas remain underdeveloped.

🏠 **Housing & settling in**
Luanda's housing market is among the world's most expensive for expats, driven by oil company demand. Rental deposits are typically 3-6 months, with annual rent often paid upfront. Secure compounds and serviced apartments dominate the expat market. Quality varies drastically — modern high-rises exist alongside crumbling infrastructure. Outside Luanda, options are limited. Most foreigners live in employer-provided housing. Landlords prefer USD or EUR payment.

💼 **Work & economy**
The economy is heavily dependent on oil (accounting for ~30% of GDP and 90% of exports), with diamonds also significant. For foreigners, opportunities center on oil and gas, mining, construction, NGO work, and increasingly tech and services as diversification efforts grow. Work permits require employer sponsorship and extensive documentation. Portuguese language is essential for most roles. Corruption and bureaucracy are realities. Salaries for expats can be high (to offset living costs) but local salaries are low.

🛂 **Visa & entry**
Visas are required for most nationalities and must be obtained before arrival (no visa on arrival). The process is slow, bureaucratic, and requires invitation letters from Angolan entities or proof of hotel bookings. Work visas require employer sponsorship, medical certificates, criminal background checks, and patience. Overstaying is taken seriously. Some nationalities face additional scrutiny.

🏥 **Healthcare**
Healthcare is a challenge outside private clinics in Luanda. Public facilities are under-resourced and overcrowded. Private clinics in Luanda serve expats and wealthy locals at high cost but with decent quality. Serious medical issues often require evacuation to South Africa or Europe. Malaria, dengue, and other tropical diseases are risks. International health insurance with evacuation coverage is essential. Life expectancy is around 61 years, reflecting healthcare limitations.

🚗 **Transport & mobility**
Luanda suffers from severe traffic congestion — commutes can take hours for short distances. Roads outside the capital vary from decent Chinese-built highways to barely passable tracks. Public transport is informal (minibus taxis called 'candongueiros') and chaotic. Most expats use private drivers or company vehicles. Domestic flights connect major cities but are expensive. International flights mainly go through Luanda's main airport, with connections to Europe, Africa, and South America.

🍛 **Food note (national dish)**
The national dish is **Moamba de Galinha**: chicken stewed in palm oil (moamba) with garlic, okra, and peppers, often served with funge (cassava flour porridge). This rich, flavorful dish reflects Angolan culinary traditions blending African ingredients with Portuguese influences.

🔎 **Bottom line**
Angola suits oil and gas professionals, mining specialists, or NGO workers on specific assignments rather than general expats or retirees. Luanda's extreme cost of living, infrastructure challenges, healthcare limitations, and visa complexity create barriers. The country offers economic opportunities in specific sectors and cultural richness, but requires employer support, high salaries to offset costs, and realistic expectations about daily challenges.

Expat Score — 4.5 / 10

national_dish.name: Moamba de Galinha
national_dish.note: Chicken stewed in palm oil with garlic, okra, and peppers, served with funge — a rich dish blending African and Portuguese influences.

lgbt_acceptance.note: Homosexuality is legal but social attitudes are conservative. Discrimination and harassment are common. Discretion is advised.

personality.name: Agostinho Neto
personality.story: Poet, physician, and revolutionary who became Angola's first president after independence in 1975. His writings inspired the liberation movement, and he's revered as the father of the nation despite leading during civil war.

cards.title: 💰 Luanda = one of world's most expensive cities
cards.text: Oil money created a distorted economy where imported goods cost multiples of global prices. A modest apartment can cost $5,000+/month. This is gradually normalizing but remains a shock for newcomers.

cards.title: 🛢️ Oil curse is real
cards.text: Despite massive oil wealth, most Angolans remain poor due to corruption, mismanagement, and lack of diversification. The inequality is staggering and visible everywhere.

cards.title: 🚗 Traffic in Luanda is legendary
cards.text: Combination of poor planning, rapid growth, limited public transport, and lack of traffic management creates hours-long gridlock. Living close to work is premium and rare.

cards.title: 🎵 Kizomba music was born here
cards.text: The sensual, romantic dance music genre Kizomba originated in Angola in the 1980s, blending traditional Semba rhythms with Caribbean zouk. It's now popular worldwide.

cards.title: 💵 Cash is king, USD is prince
cards.text: Despite official currency being kwanza, USD is widely used and preferred for major transactions. Many prices are quoted in dollars. Carrying cash is necessary as card infrastructure is limited.

cards.title: 📱 Tech leapfrogging is happening
cards.text: Mobile money and fintech are growing rapidly, allowing many to bypass traditional banking. Youth are tech-savvy despite infrastructure gaps, creating startup opportunities.
